html:
[1612]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鄰聞其女笑声，潛窥見之 - Neighbor heard the girl's laughter and secretly peeped at her.
  • 語翁妪曰 - Told the elderly couple.
  • 而子必诱致良家子与居 - Son lured a respectable young man to live with them.
  • 事倘露，祸及二老 - If the matter is revealed, disaster will befall the two elders.
  • 奈何 - What to do?
  • 翁妪因夜往觇 - Parents went to observe at night.
  • 果见女在 - Indeed saw the girl there.
  • 以爱子故，且不惊之 - Because of their love for their son, they did not frighten her.
  • 明日，呼而戒谕曰 - The next day, called her and warned her.
  • 吾不忍闻于官，令汝获罪 - We cannot bear to report to the authorities and have you suffer punishment.
  • 汝宜速绝 - You should break off the relationship quickly.
  • 不然，与其惜汝而累吾二老人，当忍情执以闻矣 - Otherwise, rather than spare you and trouble the two elders, we will have to report it despite our feelings.
  • 子不敢讳，备述前因 - Son did not hide the truth and fully described the previous events.
  • 然虽心欲绝之，而牵恋不忍 - Although wanted to break it off, could not bear to do so due to attachment.
  • 且彼亦径自至，无由可断 - Also came directly, making it impossible to end the relationship.
  • 女虽知亲责，殊不畏避 - Girl did not fear or avoid her parents' reprimand.
  • 翁妪无如之何 - Parents could do nothing about the situation.
  • 复谋之于邻，首诸官 - Consulted with the neighbor and reported to the authorities.
  • 展转达于郡守 - Passed through various hands and reached the prefect.
  • 李君守召子来 - Prefect summoned the son.
  • 不伺讯鞫，即自承伏云云 - Did not wait to interrogate and immediately admitted the son's confession.
  • 然不知其姓属居止也 - Did not know the other person's name, family, or residence.
  • 守思之，殆是妖祟，非人也 - Thought it might be a demon or evil spirit rather than a human.
  • 不下刑箠，教其子令以长线缀其衣，明日验之 - Did not use physical punishment but instructed the son to attach a long string to the other person's clothes and check the next day.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邻 - Neighbor, a common figure in classical Chinese literature.
  • 翁妪 - Elderly couple, typically the parents, who play a significant role in family dynamics.
  • 良家子 - Respectable family's son, a term used to denote someone of good social standing.
  • 官 - Authorities, including local officials and higher-ranking officials like the prefect.
  • 郡守 - Prefect, a high-ranking official in charge of a county or region.
  • 妖祟 - Demon or evil spirit, a concept often found in classical Chinese literature and folklore.

[1613]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 子受教归 - The son receives instruction and returns home.
  • 比夜入室 - By night, he enters his room.
  • 女早先知 - The woman knows in advance.
  • 袖中针线速与我 - Give me the needle and thread from your sleeve quickly.
  • 翌日，复于守 - The next day, he reports back to the guard.
  • 守曰：“今夕当以剪刀断其裙。” - The guard says, 'Tonight, I will cut her skirt with scissors.'
  • 予之剪归 - Give me the scissors and return.
  • 女复迎接，怒曰 - The woman meets him again, angry and says...
  • 奈何又要剪吾衣裙？速付剪来，茖荅贷放 - Why do you want to cut my clothes again? Give me the scissors quickly, or I will not let you go.
  • 子亟予之 - The son quickly gives it to her.
  • 又复于守 - He reports back to the guard again.
  • 守怒，即命民兵数人往取之 - The guard becomes angry and immediately orders several militia members to go and take her.
  • 兵将近其家，女先在室，知之 - As the soldiers approach her house, the woman is already in the room and knows about it.
  • 时方晴皎，忽大雨作 - It was clear and bright, but suddenly it started raining heavily.
  • 众不可前，乃返命于守 - The soldiers could not proceed, so they returned to report to the guard.
  • 守益怒，命一健邑丞帅兵数十，往以取之 - The guard becomes even more angry and orders a strong county magistrate to lead several dozen soldiers to go and take her.
  • 女亦在室，丞兵将至，忽大雷电，雨翻盆而下 - The woman is also in the room, and as the magistrate's soldiers approach, there is a sudden thunderstorm, and it rains heavily.
  • 谓火归掣，殊不能进，亦回返以告 - The fire returns, and they cannot proceed, so they also return to report.
  • 守曰：“然则任之。” - The guard says, 'Then let it be.'
  • 呼子问曰：“女之姿貌果何似？衣裳何彩色？” - He calls the son and asks, 'What does the woman look like? What colors are her clothes?'
  • 子具言“如是如是。其外内裳袂，一一皆是纻丝，悉新裁制也。” - The son answers, 'She looks like this and this. Her outer and inner clothes, sleeves, and skirts are all made of silk, and they are all newly made.'
  • 每寝解衣，垢积甚多，而前后只此，终未尝更易一件 - Every time she undresses to sleep, there is a lot of dirt, but she only has these clothes, and she has never changed them.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 子 - Refers to a young man or son, often used in classical texts to denote a male figure.
  • 女 - Refers to a woman, often used in classical texts to denote a female figure.
  • 守 - Refers to a guard or official, often used in classical texts to denote a person of authority.
  • 民兵 - Refers to militia members, often used in classical texts to denote local military forces.
  • 邑丞 - Refers to a county magistrate, often used in classical texts to denote a local government official.
  • 纻丝 - Refers to silk, often used in classical texts to denote fine fabrics.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 子受教归，比夜入室 - This sequence can be read as a single action: 'The son receives instruction and returns home, entering his room by night.'
  • 女早先知，迎谓曰 - This sequence describes the woman's reaction and her speech: 'The woman knows in advance and meets him, saying...'
  • 袖中针线速与我 - This phrase can be read as a direct request: 'Give me the needle and thread from your sleeve quickly.'
  • 翌日，复于守 - This phrase indicates a follow-up action: 'The next day, he reports back to the guard.'
  • 守曰：“今夕当以剪刀断其裙。” - This is a direct quote from the guard, indicating a threat or promise.
  • 予之剪归 - This phrase indicates an action of handing over the scissors and returning: 'Give me the scissors and return.'
  • 女复迎接，怒曰 - This phrase describes the woman's reaction and her subsequent speech: 'The woman meets him again, angry and says...'
  • 奈何又要剪吾衣裙？速付剪来，茖荅贷放 - This is a direct quote from the woman, expressing her anger and demand: 'Why do you want to cut my clothes again? Give me the scissors quickly, or I will not let you go.'
  • 子亟予之 - This phrase indicates a hurried action: 'The son quickly gives it to her.'
  • 又复于守 - This phrase indicates another follow-up action: 'He reports back to the guard again.'
  • 守怒，即命民兵数人往取之 - This phrase describes the guard's reaction and subsequent action: 'The guard becomes angry and immediately orders several militia members to go and take her.'
  • 兵将近其家，女先在室，知之 - This phrase indicates the woman's awareness of the approaching danger: 'As the soldiers approach her house, the woman is already in the room and knows about it.'
  • 时方晴皎，忽大雨作 - This phrase describes a sudden change in weather: 'It was clear and bright, but suddenly it started raining heavily.'
  • 众不可前，乃返命于守 - This phrase describes the soldiers' inability to continue and their return: 'The soldiers could not proceed, so they returned to report to the guard.'
  • 守益怒，命一健邑丞帅兵数十，往以取之 - This phrase describes the guard's escalating anger and the increased force sent: 'The guard becomes even more angry and orders a strong county magistrate to lead several dozen soldiers to go and take her.'
  • 女亦在室，丞兵将至，忽大雷电，雨翻盆而下 - This phrase describes another sudden natural event: 'The woman is also in the room, and as the magistrate's soldiers approach, there is a sudden thunderstorm, and it rains heavily.'
  • 谓火归掣，殊不能进，亦回返以告 - This phrase describes the soldiers' inability to continue due to the weather and their return: 'The fire returns, and they cannot proceed, so they also return to report.'
  • 守曰：“然则任之。” - This phrase indicates the guard's decision to give up: 'The guard says, 'Then let it be.'
  • 呼子问曰：“女之姿貌果何似？衣裳何彩色？” - This phrase describes the guard's curiosity and questioning: 'He calls the son and asks, 'What does the woman look like? What colors are her clothes?'
  • 子具言“如是如是。其外内裳袂，一一皆是纻丝，悉新裁制也。” - This is a direct quote from the son, describing the woman's appearance and clothing: 'The son answers, 'She looks like this and this. Her outer and inner clothes, sleeves, and skirts are all made of silk, and they are all newly made.'
  • 每寝解衣，垢积甚多，而前后只此，终未尝更易一件 - This phrase describes the woman's living conditions and her lack of new clothes: 'Every time she undresses to sleep, there is a lot of dirt, but she only has these clothes, and she has never changed them.'

[1614]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 密著其体 - Literal expression meaning 'closely adhering to the body,' likely referring to a tight-fitting garment.
  • 不甚解脱 - Means 'not easily taken off,' suggesting the garment is very tight.
  • 柳黄裤 - Literal expression meaning 'willow yellow trousers,' a specific type of clothing, possibly named for its color or style.
  • 衾畔 - Means 'by the quilt,' indicating the side of the bed.
  • 不暂舍也 - Means 'not temporarily set aside,' suggesting the item is kept permanently.
  • 尔去 - Means 'you leave,' a direct command.
  • 此后第接之如常时 - Means 'afterward, treat it as usual,' indicating a return to normalcy.
  • 吾自有处 - Means 'I have my own way,' suggesting a personal resolution.
  • 欲语公，恐公怒耳 - Means 'I want to tell you, but I fear you will be angry,' indicating a hesitation due to potential consequences.
  • 此人所遇之女，殆是公亡过令爱 - Means 'the woman this person met, is likely your deceased daughter,' a sensitive statement.
  • 遽起归衙 - Means 'hastily rise and return to the office,' indicating a sudden departure.
  • 急呼妻，骂守 - Means 'hastily call his wife and scold the governor,' showing a strong emotional reaction.
  • 或者果是吾家大姐乎 - Means 'perhaps it is indeed our eldest daughter,' a speculative question.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 青比甲 - A specific type of clothing, possibly a jacket or vest, likely named for its color or style.
  • 柳黄裤 - A specific type of clothing, possibly named for its color or style.
  • 衾 - Refers to a quilt or bed covering, a common item in traditional Chinese bedding.
  • 通判 - A local government official, often responsible for administrative and judicial duties.
  • 亡过令爱 - Refers to a deceased daughter, a sensitive and emotional topic in traditional Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 青比甲 - A specific type of clothing, possibly a jacket or vest.
  • 柳黄裤 - A specific type of clothing.
  • 衾 - A quilt or bed covering.
  • 通判 - A local government official.
  • 亡过令爱 - A deceased daughter.

[1615]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其容色衣饰良是也 - Literally 'her appearance and attire are indeed good,' but in context, it means that the girl appears to be a normal, well-dressed person. This is a descriptive phrase used to establish her initial normalcy.
  • 判意少解 - 'The judge’s mind was slightly relieved' or 'the judge’s resolve was somewhat softened.' This is a subtle expression of the judge’s emotional shift.
  • 其当是吾女耶 - 'Could she be my daughter?' A rhetorical question expressing doubt and emotional turmoil.
  • 第愿公勿恤之，任吾裁治可耳 - 'Just please do not be concerned, let me handle it as I see fit.' 第 here is used as a softener, similar to 'just' or 'merely,' and 可耳 is a colloquial way of saying 'that will do' or 'that is sufficient.'
  • 既而无所施设 - 'After that, there was nothing to do' or 'nothing could be done.' This indicates a period of inaction or helplessness.
  • 女来如故 - 'The girl returned as before,' meaning she continued to appear normally, despite the judge’s suspicions.
  • 有选新御史按部，事竣而去 - 'There was a newly appointed imperial censor who toured the region, and after completing his duties, he left.' This is a standard bureaucratic term referring to an official inspection.
  • 郡集弓兵二百辈护行 - 'The county gathered two hundred archers to escort him.' 弓兵 is a specific term for archers or soldiers armed with bows.
  • 守与郡耆皆送之野 - 'The county magistrate and the elders of the county all saw him off at the outskirts.' 郡耆 refers to the senior citizens or elders of the county.
  • 守遂道从东门以归 - 'The magistrate then led the way through the eastern gate and returned.' This describes a deliberate and controlled movement.
  • 至桃园，守驻车，麾兵悉入园 - 'Upon reaching the peach garden, the magistrate stopped his chariot and ordered the soldiers to enter the garden.' 驻车 is a formal term for halting the chariot.
  • 即令起判女冢 - 'He immediately ordered the excavation of the judge’s daughter’s tomb.' 冢 is a term for a tomb or grave.
  • 视之，女棺之前，有一窍如指大，四围莹滑，若有物久出入者 - 'Upon inspecting it, there was a hole in front of the coffin, about the size of a finger, smooth and polished around the edges, as if something had been entering and exiting for a long time.' This is a detailed and eerie description of the tomb.
  • 即斫棺，视女貌如生 - 'He then cut open the coffin and saw the girl’s face, as if she were still alive.' 斫棺 is a strong verb meaning to chop or break open a coffin.
  • 因举而焚之 - 'He then lifted her up and burned her.' This is a dramatic and final action.
  • 盖守知女已鬼能神 - 'It was because the magistrate knew that his daughter had become a ghost and had gained supernatural powers.' 盖 is a conjunction meaning 'it was because' or 'the reason is that.'
  • 故寝其事，乘其不知而忽举 - 'Therefore, he concealed the matter and, taking advantage of her unawareness, suddenly acted.' 寝其事 means to hide or keep the matter secret.
  • 鬼果不能为也 - 'Indeed, the ghost could not do anything.' This is a conclusion drawn from the magistrate’s successful action.
  • 守恐鬼气侵子深，或复来殢 - 'The magistrate feared that the ghost’s influence on his son was too deep, and that it might return to haunt him.' 殢 means to haunt or trouble persistently.
  • 召入郡中。令守郡聚，与同役者直宿，三月无悉，乃释之 - 'He summoned the son into the county, ordered him to stay in the county compound, and to share watch duties with other officials. After three months with no further incidents, he was released.' 直宿 is a term for night duty or guard duty.
  • 其怪遂绝 - 'The strange phenomenon then ceased.' A concise way of saying that the supernatural events stopped.
  • 后子亦竟无他 - 'Later, the son also had no further issues.' A final statement confirming that the problem was resolved.
  • 事在弘治中也 - 'This event took place during the Hongzhi era.' 弘治 is the reign title of the Ming emperor Hongzhi, and it is a proper noun that must be translated consistently.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 判 - This is a classical term for a judge or magistrate, often used in legal and administrative contexts in ancient China.
  • 守 - This refers to a county magistrate or local official, often used interchangeably with 判 depending on the context.
  • 御史 - A high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy, responsible for supervision, inspection, and justice. The term 按部 refers to the duty of touring and inspecting local areas.
  • 弓兵 - Literally 'archer soldiers,' a specific type of military or local defense force in ancient China.
  • 郡耆 - Refers to the elders or senior citizens of the county, who often played a role in local governance and community affairs.
  • 桃园 - 'Peach garden' is a common name for a place, but it may also carry symbolic or metaphorical meanings, such as a place of transformation or hidden truths.
  • 冢 - A term for a tomb or grave, often used in classical texts to denote the resting place of the deceased.
  • 鬼能神 - 'Ghost who has become a spirit' or 'ghost with supernatural powers.' This is a common expression in Chinese folklore to describe a ghost that has gained supernatural abilities.
  • 寝其事 - To conceal or keep a matter secret, a term used in both legal and personal contexts.
  • 直宿 - A duty or watch shift, often performed by officials or guards to ensure security or vigilance.
  • 弘治 - The reign title of the Ming emperor Hongzhi (r. 1488–1505), a period known for relative stability and cultural flourishing in China.

[1616]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乾道初 - Refers to the early years of the Qian Dao era during the Song Dynasty.
  • 才貌逸群 - Means 'talented and good-looking beyond the ordinary.'
  • 托迹江湖 - Means 'to take refuge in the rivers and lakes,' suggesting a life of wandering and seclusion.
  • 抚景自适 - Means 'to enjoy the scenery and find one's own comfort.'
  • 命傒僮焚香 - Refers to ordering a servant to burn incense, a common practice for setting a peaceful atmosphere.
  • 鼓琴于篷窗之下 - Describes playing the guqin (a traditional Chinese zither) under the window of a boat.
  • 极尽绸缪 - Means 'to enjoy each other thoroughly.'
  • 铭刻心骨 - Means 'to engrave in one's heart and bones.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乾道 - The era name of Emperor Xiaozong of Song (1165-1189).
  • 清河 - An ancient county in Hebei Province, known for its cultural significance.
  • 两淮 - Refers to the regions of Jiangsu and Anhui provinces, historically important for salt production.
  • 盐运使 - A high-ranking official responsible for the administration of salt production and distribution.
  • 翠薇 - The given name of the woman, which may have cultural or symbolic significance.
  • 书亭 - A study pavilion, often a place for reading and writing.
  • 《忆秦娥》 - A classical Chinese poem form, often associated with love and nostalgia.

Proper Nouns

  • 乾道 - The era name of Emperor Xiaozong of Song.
  • 清河 - An ancient county in Hebei Province.
  • 邱任 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 两淮 - The regions of Jiangsu and Anhui provinces.
  • 盐运使 - The title of the official.
  • 何公 - The name of the official.
  • 翠薇 - The given name of the woman.
  • 《忆秦娥》 - The title of the poem.

[1617]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 香篆袅 - Classical phrase describing the slow, lingering smoke of incense, evoking a serene or romantic atmosphere.
  • 罗帏锦帐 - Refers to luxurious, embroidered curtains and canopies, symbolizing wealth and comfort.
  • 风光好 - Literally means 'good scenery,' but in this context, conveys a sense of pleasant, idyllic surroundings.
  • 金钗斜軃 - Describes a woman's hairpin falling slightly, suggesting a moment of intimacy or distraction.
  • 夙颠鸾倒 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'long-cherished desires fulfilled' or 'long-awaited moments of passion.'
  • 蓬莱岛 - Refers to the mythical island of Penglai, a symbol of paradise or an idealized, otherworldly place.
  • 缘不小 - Means 'the connection is significant' or 'the bond is meaningful.'
  • 最关情处 - Refers to the most emotionally significant or touching moment.
  • 娥眉淡扫 - Describes a woman's slightly applied makeup, suggesting a natural, understated beauty.
  • 杨枝袅 - Refers to the graceful swaying of willow branches, often used as a metaphor for elegance or grace.
  • 恩情无限天将晓 - Means 'boundless affection as dawn approaches,' suggesting a moment of deep emotional connection just before parting.
  • 漏穷鸡唤 - Refers to the sound of a rooster crowing at dawn, signaling the end of the night.
  • 教人烦恼 - Means 'to cause distress' or 'to bring worry.'
  • 邮亭一夜风沙少 - Refers to a night spent at a roadside inn, with the wind and sand being less harsh, suggesting a brief respite.
  • 匆匆后会应难保 - Means 'a hasty farewell with uncertain future meetings,' expressing the sadness of a temporary separation.
  • 最伤情处 - Refers to the most emotionally painful moment.
  • 残云风扫 - Describes scattered clouds being blown away by the wind, symbolizing the fleeting nature of moments.
  • 不断尘缘露本真 - Means 'the ties of worldly life reveal one's true nature,' suggesting that even in the midst of worldly connections, one's true self is exposed.
  • 翠薇花下绕香魂 - Refers to a spirit wandering among the fragrant flowers of the cypress, evoking a sense of ethereal beauty and longing.
  • 如今了却风流愿 - Means 'now fulfilling one's romantic wishes,' suggesting the completion of a desire for love or passion.
  • 一任东风啼鸟声 - Refers to the sound of birds singing in the east wind, symbolizing the passage of time and the inevitability of change.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蓬莱岛 - The mythical island of Penglai, often associated with immortality and paradise in Chinese mythology.
  • 娥眉淡扫 - A reference to the traditional Chinese practice of applying light makeup, symbolizing natural beauty and elegance.
  • 翠薇花 - Refers to the cypress flower, often associated with longevity and elegance in Chinese culture.
  • 风流愿 - Refers to romantic or passionate desires, often associated with the literati and their pursuit of love and beauty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '香篆袅，罗帏锦帐风光好。风光好，金钗斜軃，夙颠鸾倒。' must be read as a single unit, describing a scene of luxury and passion.
  • The sequence '恍疑身在蓬莱岛，邂逅相逢缘不小。缘不小，最关情处，娥眉淡扫。' shifts from a description of the setting to a reflection on the emotional significance of the moment.
  • The sequence '杨枝袅，恩情无限天将晓。天将晓，漏穷鸡唤，教人烦恼。' describes the emotional complexity of the moment, blending beauty with the inevitability of parting.
  • The sequence '邮亭一夜风沙少，匆匆后会应难保。应难保，最伤情处，残云风扫。' reflects on the transient nature of the encounter and the sadness of separation.
  • The sequence '不断尘缘露本真，翠薇花下绕香魂。如今了却风流愿，一任东风啼鸟声。' concludes with a reflection on the fulfillment of romantic desires and the passage of time.

[1618]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生惊怅久之 - Describes a state of prolonged shock and sadness.
  • 明日复访故处 - The next day, the person revisited the same place.
  • 惟见空亭幽寂，景物萧然，杳无人迹 - Only see an empty pavilion, quiet and secluded, with desolate scenery and no traces of people.
  • 就询庄妪 - To go and ask an old woman.
  • 此我主人何公书亭也 - This is the pavilion belonging to Master He.
  • 主人有妾名翠薇，工画琴，善诗赋，我主甚爱之，为主妇妒而鸩死 - My master has a concubine named Cui Wei, skilled in painting and playing the qin, good at poetry and prose, loved very much by my master, but poisoned to death by the jealousy of the main wife.
  • 主人恸惜，瘗此亭左，环植薇花，其认之 - The master was deeply grieved, buried her to the left of this pavilion, surrounded by violets, you can recognize it.
  • 君昨遇者，毋乃此乎 - What you met yesterday, isn't it this?
  • 精爽依稀逼太真，何缘月下觑芳魂 - Spirit vaguely resembling the ideal beauty, why under the moon to see the soul of beauty.
  • 清风一阵浑无迹，惟听流泉鸣咽声 - A gust of clear wind leaves no trace, only hear the sobbing sound of flowing spring water.
  • 复奠其冢而返 - To offer a sacrifice again at her tomb and then returned.
  • 某枢密使女 - The daughter of a high-ranking official.
  • 某州郡学倪升，成化丁酉，假读一僧舍 - Nisi, a student in a school in a certain state or prefecture, borrowed a room in a temple during the Chenghua era.
  • 壁间忽辟双扉，升讶之曰 - Suddenly opened two doors in the wall, Nisi was surprised and said.
  • 尔谓红叶之约，可也。谓绿绮之奔，妾岂文君比哉 - You say the red leaf pact, that's fine. You say the green silk run, how can I compare to Wenjun?
  • 慎勿泄露。终当为箕帚妾耳 - Be careful not to reveal it. You will eventually become my housewife.
  • 乃赋诗二律曰 - Then she composed two regulated verses and said.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 生惊怅久之 - Reflects the emotional state of the narrator after encountering a mysterious figure.
  • 明日复访故处 - The act of revisiting the same place the next day suggests a desire to understand or confirm the previous encounter.
  • 惟见空亭幽寂，景物萧然，杳无人迹 - Sets a somber and isolated atmosphere.

  • 香魂与腻骨，消散如黄埃 - Describes the fragrance and flesh of贞娘 as dissipating like yellow dust, suggesting the impermanence of physical beauty.
  • 何事千百年，一名长在哉 - A rhetorical question asking why贞娘's name has endured for centuries.

  • 无色故无名，丘冢空崔嵬 - States that those without beauty or talent are forgotten and their graves are left empty and desolate.
  • 惟有贞娘墓，客到情徘徊 - Emphasizes that only贞娘's tomb evokes a lingering emotion when visitors come.
  • 我是好名者，尔尔倾一杯 - A self-deprecating statement indicating that the writer is a lover of fame and pours a cup of wine to贞娘.
  • 我非好色者，后人无相咍 - A statement indicating that the writer is not a lover of beauty and hopes that future generations will not mock him.
  • 噫！元之非好色，何为倾此一杯？合曰“好名”，名从何来？此自讳其情而不能者也 - A concluding remark questioning why Wang Yuanzhi poured a cup of wine if he was not a lover of beauty, suggesting that he was actually a lover of fame and could not admit it.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 別離情緒 - Literal meaning 'parting emotions,' a common expression referring to feelings of separation or longing.
  • 奈一番好景，一番愁戚 - 'Nai' is an archaic particle meaning 'how' or 'what,' often used for rhetorical emphasis. '一番' means 'a bout' or 'a time,' and '好景' refers to beautiful scenery or good times. '愁戚' means sorrow or grief.
  • 燕語鶯啼人乍遠 - A poetic phrase describing the sounds of swallows and orioles, symbolizing the departure of someone dear. '乍遠' means 'just distant' or 'suddenly far away.'
  • 還是他鄉寒食 - '寒食' refers to the Cold Food Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday. '他鄉' means 'in a foreign land' or 'away from home.'
  • 桃李無言，不堪攀折 - A metaphorical expression from the *Analects* of Confucius, meaning that even though peach and plum trees do not speak, their fruits bear witness to their virtues. '不堪攀折' means 'cannot be picked' or 'cannot be taken.'
  • 總是風流客 - '風流' can mean 'charismatic' or 'elegant,' and '客' means 'guest' or 'traveler.' Together, it refers to someone who is a traveler or guest in life.
  • 東君也自怪人 - '東君' is a poetic name for the god of spring or the sun. '怪人' means 'to blame someone' or 'to find someone strange.'
  • 冷淡蹤跡 - Means 'cold and distant' or 'indifferent and aloof.'
  • 花艷草芳春事 - Describes the beauty of flowers and grass during spring. '春事' means 'spring affairs' or 'spring activities.'
  • 每隨花意薄，疏狂狼籍 - '花意薄' means 'the meaning of flowers is thin' or 'flowers are fleeting.' '疏狂' means 'reckless' or 'wild.' '狼籍' means 'in disarray' or 'chaotic.'
  • 除卻清風並皓月 - '除卻' means 'except for' or 'apart from.' '清風' means 'clear breeze,' and '皓月' means 'bright moon.'
  • 脈脈此情誰識 - '脈脈' means 'deeply' or 'profoundly.' '此情' means 'this feeling' or 'this emotion.' '誰識' means 'who understands' or 'who knows.'
  • 料得文君，重簾不卷，只等閒消息 - '料得' means 'suppose' or 'expect.' '文君' refers to Sima Xiangru's wife, a famous literary figure. '重簾不卷' means 'thick curtains not lifted' or 'curtains not opened.' '等閒消息' means 'ordinary news' or 'trivial information.'
  • 不如歸去，受他真個憐惜 - '不如' means 'it's better to' or 'it's better not to.' '歸去' means 'return home.' '受他真個憐惜' means 'receive genuine care' or 'be truly cared for.'

  • 平吴之后，复纳孙皓宫人数千 - After conquering Wu, he took in thousands of concubines from the palace of Sun Hao, where '平吴' means 'conquering Wu' and '复纳' means 'took in again'.
  • 自此掖庭殆将万人，而并宠者甚众 - From then on, the imperial harem nearly numbered ten thousand, with many being favored, where '掖庭' refers to the imperial harem and '并宠' means 'being favored together'.
  • 帝莫知所适，常乘羊车，恣其所之，至便宴寝 - The emperor did not know where to go, so he often rode in a sheep-drawn carriage, allowing it to go wherever it pleased, and would rest and sleep there, where '莫知所适' means 'did not know where to go' and '恣其所之' means 'allowed it to go wherever it pleased'.
  • 宫人乃取竹叶插户，以盐汁洒地，而引帝车 - The palace women would take bamboo leaves and insert them into the door, sprinkle salt water on the ground, and thus attract the emperor's carriage, where '取竹叶插户' means 'take bamboo leaves and insert them into the door' and '以盐汁洒地' means 'sprinkle salt water on the ground'.
  • 然芳最蒙爱幸，殆有专房之宠焉 - However, Fang was most favored and loved, almost having exclusive favor, where '最蒙爱幸' means 'most favored and loved' and '专房之宠' means 'exclusive favor'.
  • 侍御服饰，亚于皇后 - Her attendants' attire was second only to that of the empress, where '亚于皇后' means 'second only to that of the empress'.
  • 帝尝与之樗蒲，争矢，遂伤上指 - The emperor once played dice with her, and they quarreled over the stakes, causing him to injure his finger, where '争矢' means 'quarreled over the stakes'.
  • 此固将种也 - This is indeed the seed of a general, where '将种' means 'seed of a general'.
  • 北伐公孙，西距诸葛，非将种而何 - Defeating Gong Sun and resisting Zhuge, how could this not be the seed of a general? where '北伐公孙' means 'defeating Gong Sun' and '西距诸葛' means 'resisting Zhuge'.
  • 帝甚有惭色 - The emperor was very embarrassed, where '甚有惭色' means 'very embarrassed'.
  • 宋文帝潘淑妃者，本以貌进，始未见赏 - Pan Shufei of Emperor Wen of Song, originally advanced due to her beauty, was not initially favored, where '本以貌进' means 'originally advanced due to her beauty' and '始未见赏' means 'was not initially favored'.
  • 帝曰：“羊乃为汝徘徊，况人乎？” - The emperor said, 'The sheep lingers for you; how much more so for people?' where '羊乃为汝徘徊' means 'the sheep lingers for you'.
  • 开元遗事 - Tales of the Kaiyuan Era, where '开元' refers to the Kaiyuan era of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 开元中，明皇每至春时，且暮宴于宫中 - During the Kaiyuan era, Emperor Ming Huang often held evening banquets in the palace during spring, where '且暮宴于宫中' means 'held evening banquets in the palace'.
  • 又为彩局儿：集宫嫔用骰子掷，最胜一人乃得专夜 - He also set up a colorful game: gathering palace concubines to play dice, the one who won could have the night alone, where '彩局儿' means 'colorful game' and '最胜一人乃得专夜' means 'the one who won could have the night alone'.
  • 宦珰私号骰子为“剉角媒人” - The eunuchs privately called the dice 'angle-grinding matchmaker', where '剉角媒人' means 'angle-grinding matchmaker'.
  • 又或投金钱赌侍帝寝 - Sometimes they would bet money to determine who would serve the emperor in bed, where '投金钱赌侍帝寝' means 'bet money to determine who would serve the emperor in bed'.
  • 自贵妃入，遂罢此戏 - After the favorite concubine entered, the game was discontinued, where '自贵妃入' means 'after the favorite concubine entered'.

  • 帝莫知所适，常乘羊车，恣其所之，至便宴寝 - Refers to the practice of the emperor using a sheep-drawn carriage to choose his concubines, a famous anecdote from the Jin Dynasty.

  • 北伐公孙，西距诸葛，非将种而何 - Refers to the historical context of the Three Kingdoms period, where the concubine's ancestors were involved in military campaigns.
  • 帝甚有惭色 - Refers to the emperor's embarrassment, indicating his regret or shame.
  • 宋文帝潘淑妃者，本以貌进，始未见赏 - Refers to Pan Shufei, a concubine of Emperor Wen of Song, who was initially not favored despite her beauty.
  • 帝曰：“羊乃为汝徘徊，况人乎？” - Refers to the emperor's comment on the sheep's behavior, implying that people would also linger for the concubine.
  • 开元遗事 - Refers to the Kaiyuan era of the Tang Dynasty, a period of prosperity and cultural flourishing.
  • 开元中，明皇每至春时，且暮宴于宫中 - Refers to the practice of Emperor Ming Huang holding evening banquets in the palace during spring, a common pastime for the imperial court.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 申王每醉 - Formulaic expression meaning 'Whenever the Prince of Shen was drunk,' setting up a habitual action.
  • 即使宫妓将锦彩结一兜子 - Complex construction meaning 'he would have the palace musicians weave a pouch from silk and brocade.' The verb 将 here functions as a causative.
  • 令宫妓舁归寝室 - 'He would have the palace musicians carry it back to his bedroom.' �舁 means to carry on a palanquin or shoulder.
  • 本宫呼曰醉舆 - 'The palace referred to it as the Drunken Palanquin.' 呼曰 is a standard phrase for 'called it.'
  • 使宫妓密围于坐侧，以御寒气 - 'He would have the palace musicians closely surround him on the side of his seat to guard against the cold.' 御寒气 is a classical expression for 'ward off the cold.'
  • 自呼为妓围 - 'He called it the Musician Enclosure.' 自呼为 means 'called it himself.'
  • 笞妓 - Heading meaning 'Whipping the Musician' or 'Punishing the Musician.' This is a title or section heading.
  • 吕士隆知宣州 - 'Lü Shilong served as the governor of Xuanzhou.' 知 is a classical term for holding an official post.
  • 好苔营妓 - Phrase that is ambiguous. 苔营妓 may be a misspelling or misreading. The most likely intended term is 好调营妓, meaning 'enjoyed selecting or entertaining musicians.' 调营妓 is a classical term for selecting or arranging for musicians.
  • 会杭州一妓到 - 'When a musician from Hangzhou arrived.' 会 here means 'when' or 'at the time when.'
  • 妾不敢辞罪，但恐杭妓不能安 - 'I dare not plead innocence, but I fear that the Hangzhou musician will not be at ease.' 辞罪 is a formal expression for 'plead guilty' or 'deny the crime.'
  • 谢豹 - Title or story heading meaning 'The Cheetah' or 'The Leopard,' likely a nickname or title for the *Zizhi* (a bird, the cuckoo).
  • 锦城谢家 - 'The Xie family of Jincheng.' 锦城 is a poetic name for Chengdu, 谢家 refers to a family by the surname Xie.
  • 其女窥而悦之 - 'His daughter secretly admired him.' 窥 means to look at secretly or from a distance.
  • 子规啼 - Refers to the call of the *zizhi* bird, commonly known as the cuckoo, which is also called *谢豹* in this story.
  • 怔忡若豹鸣也 - 'She became agitated as if a leopard were howling.' 怔忡 is a term for emotional disturbance or anxiety.
  • 使侍女以竹枝驱之曰 - 'She had her maidservant drive it away with a bamboo branch, saying...' 驱之 is to drive it away.
  • 豹，汝尚敢至此啼乎？ - 'Leopard, you still dare to cry here?' A rhetorical question.
  • 故名子规为谢豹 - 'Hence, the *zizhi* bird was named *Xie Bao*.' 故名 is a classical expression for 'henceforth known as.'
  • 出《成都旧事》 - 'Recorded in *The Old Tales of Chengdu*.' 出 means 'recorded in' or 'taken from.'
  • 《酉阳杂俎》以杜鹃知阳，相推而鸣，先鸣者吐血死；初鸣时，先听者主离别 - Complex sentence from *Yuyang Zazu* (a classical text). 知阳 likely means 'knows the sun' or 'knows the time of day.' 相推而鸣 – 'they call out in succession.' 先鸣者吐血死 – 'the one who calls first dies spitting blood.' 初鸣时，先听者主离别 – 'At the first cry, the one who hears it first is fated to experience parting.'
  • 盖不祥之鸟也 - 'It is a bird of ill omen.' 盖 is a classical particle meaning 'it is' or 'this is.'
  • 选婿窗 - Title meaning 'The Window for Choosing a Husband.'
  • 情史 - Title of the collection, meaning 'History of Feelings' or 'History of Love.'
  • （明）詹詹外史 评辑 - (Ming) Zhanzhan Wai Shi, compiler and annotator. 评辑 means 'compiled and annotated.'

[1737]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 男女相悦为婚 - Classical phrase meaning 'men and women who are fond of each other marry,' introducing the topic of marriage.
  • 良法也 - Means 'a good law' or 'a good practice,' indicating that marriage is a positive social institution.
  • 吾女各有姿色 - Means 'my daughters each have their own beauty.'
  • 即不知谁是匹耦 - Means 'I don't know who would be a suitable match.'
  • 吾令五女各持一丝幔前 - Means 'I will have my five daughters each hold a thread in front of the curtain.'
  • 子自牵之 - Means 'you will choose one of them.'
  • 得者为婿 - Means 'whoever you choose will be your wife.'
  • 美风姿 - Describes someone as having a beautiful appearance or demeanor.
  • 有才艺 - Means 'has talent and skills.'
  • 宰相张嘉贞欲纳为婿 - Means 'Premier Zhang Jiazheng wanted to take him as a son-in-law.'
  • 遂牵一红丝线 - Means 'he then pulled a red thread.'
  • 得第三女 - Means 'and chose the third daughter.'
  • 大有姿色 - Means 'who is very beautiful.'
  • 待阙鸳鸯社 - Metaphorical term referring to a place where young couples wait for their turn to marry.
  • 朱子春未婚 - Means 'Zhu Zichun was unmarried.'
  • 先开房室 - Means 'he prepared a room in advance.'
  • 帷帐甚丽 - Means 'with very beautiful curtains and screens.'
  • 以待其事 - Means 'to wait for his wedding.'
  • 时人谓之待阙鸳鸯社 - Means 'people at the time called it the 'Waiting Room for Lovebirds.'
  • 见《妆楼记》 - Means 'as recorded in *Zhuang Lou Ji*.'
  • 鸟之鸣春，虫之鸣秋 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'birds sing in spring, insects sing in autumn,' used to describe natural phenomena.
  • 情也 - Means 'it is emotion.'
  • 迫于时而不自已 - Means 'driven by the times and unable to stop.'
  • 时往而情亦遁矣 - Means 'when the times pass, so does the emotion.'
  • 人则不然 - Means 'but humans are different.'
  • 韵之为诗，协之为词 - Means 'expressing emotions through poetry and harmonizing them into lyrics.'
  • 一日之讴吟叹咏 - Means 'daily singing and reciting.'
  • 垂之千百世而不废 - Means 'lasting for thousands of years without being forgotten.'
  • 其事之关情者 - Means 'the matters concerning emotions.'
  • 则又传为美谈 - Means 'are also passed down as beautiful stories.'
  • 笔之小牍 - Means 'written in small documents.'
  • 后世诵其诗，歌其词 - Means 'future generations recite his poems and sing his lyrics.'
  • 述其事 - Means 'narrate his stories.'
  • 而想见其情 - Means 'and imagine his emotions.'
  • 当日之是非邪正 - Means 'the right and wrong of that day.'
  • 亦因是而有所考也 - Means 'can also be judged from this.'
  • 人以情传 - Means 'people are known through their emotions.'
  • 情则何负于人矣 - Means 'emotions have nothing to blame on people.'
  • 情以人蔽 - Means 'emotions are obscured by people.'
  • 奈何自负其情耶 - Means 'how can one blame their own emotions?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 郭元振 - Historical figure from the Tang Dynasty, known for his military and political achievements.
  • 张嘉贞 - Prominent official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his administrative skills.
  • 辛稼轩 - Xin Qiji, a renowned poet and military leader from the Song Dynasty, known for his contributions to Chinese literature.
  • 《妆楼记》 - A historical record or literary work, possibly a collection of stories or anecdotes.
  • 情史氏 - A commentary heading, likely a pseudonym or title used by the author to introduce a reflective or evaluative section.

Proper Nouns

  • 郭元振 - Guo Yuanzhen, a historical figure.
  • 张嘉贞 - Zhang Jiazheng, a historical figure.
  • 辛稼轩 - Xin Qiji, a historical figure.
  • 田田 - Tiantian, one of Xin Qiji's concubines.
  • 钱钱 - Qianqian, one of Xin Qiji's concubines.
  • 《妆楼记》 - A historical or literary work.
